[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Yiftach+Brigade%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Yiftach+Brigade%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Yiftach+Brigade%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Yiftach+Brigade%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(January 2013)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(also known as the **Yiftach Brigade**) is a reserve formation of the [Israel Defense Forces](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces "Israel Defense Forces"), composed mainly of fighters that completed their compulsory service in the [Unit 621 – 'Egoz'](/wiki/Egoz_Unit "Egoz Unit"). The brigade is assigned to the [99th Infantry Division](/wiki/99th_Infantry_Division_(Israel) "99th Infantry Division (Israel)").
[Yiftach Brigade](https://palmach.org.il/history/database/?itemId=6191) in the Palmach website (Hebrew). See also pages for the specific battalions (גדודים) there.
[The end of the campaign against disbanding the Palmach](https://palmach.org.il/history/database/?itemId=5278) in the Palmach website (Hebrew).

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Unit of the Israel Defense Forces

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0d/YiftachBrigade.svg/250px-YiftachBrigade.svg.png)](/wiki/File:YiftachBrigade.svg)

Emblem of the brigade

The **11th Brigade** (also known as the **Yiftach Brigade**) is a reserve formation of the [Israel Defense Forces](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces "Israel Defense Forces"), composed mainly of fighters that completed their compulsory service in the [Unit 621 – 'Egoz'](/wiki/Egoz_Unit "Egoz Unit"). The brigade is assigned to the [99th Infantry Division](/wiki/99th_Infantry_Division_(Israel) "99th Infantry Division (Israel)").

## History

In the [1948 Arab–Israeli War](/wiki/1948_Arab%E2%80%93Israeli_War "1948 Arab–Israeli War") it was a [Palmach](/wiki/Palmach "Palmach") [infantry](/wiki/Infantry "Infantry") brigade. It was formed in late April 1948 from existing Palmach battalions: the First Battalion, that resided in [Jezreel Valley](/wiki/Jezreel_Valley "Jezreel Valley") and the Third Battalion in the eastern valleys. In late 1948, the Second Palmach Battalion was transferred to it from the [Negev Brigade](/wiki/Negev_Brigade "Negev Brigade").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

The Palmach memorial website records 274 of its members being killed whilst in the Yiftach Brigade.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In May 1949 the Palmach brigade was disbanded. This was part of a general downsizing of the IDF (9 out of 12 brigades were disbanded). It was also considered by many as part of Ben-Gurion's process of disbanding the Palmach itself.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

A while after that, it was re-established as a reserve unit that served in the [Gaza Strip](/wiki/Gaza_Strip "Gaza Strip") front[[3]](#cite_note-yad_lashirion-3)[[4]](#cite_note-yad_lashirion_archived-4) As an infantry brigade it also fought in the [Suez Crisis](/wiki/Suez_Crisis "Suez Crisis") and in the [Six-Day War](/wiki/Six-Day_War "Six-Day War") in the Gaza Strip[[4]](#cite_note-yad_lashirion_archived-4)[[3]](#cite_note-yad_lashirion-3)

In 1971 the brigade was converted to an armored brigade. It fought in the Egyptian front of the [Yom Kippur War](/wiki/Yom_Kippur_War "Yom Kippur War") in 1973. After the war it was regrouped and its unit number was changed to 576. in 2006 the brigade fought in [Operation Defensive Shield](/wiki/Operation_Defensive_Shield "Operation Defensive Shield").[[3]](#cite_note-yad_lashirion-3)

In May 2014 it was disbanded. [[3]](#cite_note-yad_lashirion-3)

### Reestablishment

In 2016, the decision was made to reestablish the brigade as a reserves elite infantry force by transferring battalions from the 810th “Hermon” Brigade that was disbanded as part of the multi-year Gideon Plan. Nearly all of the soldiers in the brigade served as operators in the elite Egoz unit. In September 2020, the brigade transferred command to the [99th Infantry Division](/wiki/99th_Infantry_Division_(Israel) "99th Infantry Division (Israel)"), where it serves as a commando reserves force.

## Organization

* [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0d/YiftachBrigade.svg/40px-YiftachBrigade.svg.png)](/wiki/File:YiftachBrigade.svg) **11th Infantry Brigade "Yiftach"**
  + 5037th Infantry Battalion
  + 7220th Infantry Battalion
  + 8226th Infantry Battalion
  + (7810th) Reconnaissance Battalion
  + 5011th Logistics Battalion
  + Signals Company

## Military operations

The Yiftach Brigade participated in the following Israeli military operations:

* [Operation Yiftach](/wiki/Operation_Yiftach "Operation Yiftach")
* [Operation Yoram](/w/index.php?title=Operation_Yoram&action=edit&redlink=1 "Operation Yoram (page does not exist)")
* [Operation Danny](/wiki/Operation_Danny "Operation Danny")
* [Operation Yoav](/wiki/Operation_Yoav "Operation Yoav")
* [Metzudat Koach](/wiki/Metzudat_Koach "Metzudat Koach")

## Memorial

The memorial for the fallen soldiers of the Yiftach Brigade is situated in the northern Negev north of [Rahat](/wiki/Rahat "Rahat"), near [Kibbutz](/wiki/Kibbutz "Kibbutz") [Beit Kama](/wiki/Beit_Kama "Beit Kama") and Kama Junction on [Road 40](/wiki/Highway_40_(Israel) "Highway 40 (Israel)").

## See also

* [List of battles and operations in the 1948 Palestine war](/wiki/List_of_battles_and_operations_in_the_1948_Palestine_war "List of battles and operations in the 1948 Palestine war")
